approve(address,uint256).details: Gives permission to `to` to transfer `tokenId` token to another account. The approval is cleared when the token is transferred. Only a single account can be approved at a time, so approving the zero address clears previous approvals. Requirements: - The caller must own the token or be an approved operator. - `tokenId` must exist. Emits an {Approval} event.

balanceOf(address).details: Returns the number of tokens in ``owner``'s account.

borrowFrom(uint256,address,uint256,uint2.details: The caller must first grant BURN_TOKENS permission to this contract via JBPermissions.setPermissionsFor(). This is required because collateral posting burns the caller's tokens through the controller.Collateral tokens are permanently burned when the loan is created. They are re-minted to the borrower only upon repayment. If the loan expires (after LOAN_LIQUIDATION_DURATION), the collateral is permanently lost and cannot be recovered.A delegated operator (with OPEN_LOAN permission) can set `beneficiary` to any address, directing borrowed funds away from the holder. Holders should only grant OPEN_LOAN to fully trusted operators.

params.beneficiary: The address that will receive the borrowed funds and the tokens resulting from fee payments.
params.collateralCount: The amount of tokens to use as collateral for the loan.
params.minBorrowAmount: The minimum amount to borrow, denominated in `token`.
params.prepaidFeePercent: The fee percent to charge upfront. Prepaying a fee is cheaper than paying later.
params.revnetId: The ID of the revnet to borrow from.
params.token: The token to borrow from the revnet's canonical multi terminal.

returns.loanId: The ID of the loan created.

params.collateralCount: The amount of collateral to secure the loan with.
params.currency: The currency to denominate the resulting amount in.
params.decimals: The decimals to use for the resulting fixed point value.
params.revnetId: The ID of the revnet to borrow from.

returns.borrowableCapacity: The economic ceiling for the collateral, including amounts already borrowed against this revnet. This is the value used when valuing existing collateral while repaying or reallocating a loan.
returns.borrowableNow: The amount a borrow can execute right now — the smaller of the economic ceiling and the terminal's live balance. Opening a borrow draws from the terminal's live balance, so this never exceeds what the terminal can disburse in the current state.

params.controller: The controller that manages revnets using this loans contract.
params.owner: The owner of the contract that can set the URI resolver.
params.revId: The ID of the REV revnet that will receive the fees.
params.suckerRegistry: The registry used to discover peer chain suckers for cross-chain supply/surplus awareness.
params.terminal: The canonical payout terminal that holds revnet treasury balances and sources loans.
params.trustedForwarder: A trusted forwarder of transactions to this contract.

params.loan: The loan to determine the source fee for.

returns._0: sourceFeeAmount The source fee amount for the loan.

getApproved(uint256).details: Returns the account approved for `tokenId` token. Requirements: - `tokenId` must exist.

isApprovedForAll(address,address).details: Returns if the `operator` is allowed to manage all of the assets of `owner`. See {setApprovalForAll}

isTrustedForwarder(address).details: Indicates whether any particular address is the trusted forwarder.

liquidateExpiredLoansFrom(uint256,uint25.details: Liquidation permanently destroys the collateral backing expired loans. Since collateral tokens were burned at deposit time (not held in escrow), there is nothing to return upon liquidation -- the collateral count is simply removed from tracking. The borrower retains whatever funds they received from the loan, but the collateral tokens that were burned to secure the loan are permanently lost.This is an intentional design choice to keep the protocol simple and to incentivize timely repayment or refinancing. Borrowers have the full LOAN_LIQUIDATION_DURATION (10 years) to repay their loan and recover their collateral via re-minting.Since some loans may be reallocated or paid off, loans within startingLoanId and startingLoanId + count may be skipped, so choose these parameters carefully to avoid extra gas usage.

params.count: The number of loans to iterate over.
params.revnetId: The ID of the revnet to liquidate loans from.
params.startingLoanId: The loan number to start iterating from.

params.loanId: The ID of the loan to look up.

loanSourceTokensOf(uint256).details: This array only grows -- sources are never removed. The number of distinct sources is practically bounded by the number of accepted token accounting contexts, which is typically small.

params.revnetId: The ID of the revnet to look up.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

ownerOf(uint256).details: Returns the owner of the `tokenId` token. Requirements: - `tokenId` must exist.

reallocateCollateralFromLoan(uint256,uin.details: Useful if a loan's collateral has gone up in value since the loan was created.Refinancing a loan will burn the original and create two new loans.This function is intentionally not payable — it only moves existing collateral between loans and does not accept new funds. Any ETH sent with the call will be rejected by the EVM.A delegated operator (with REALLOCATE_LOAN permission) can set `beneficiary` to any address, directing borrowed funds from the new loan away from the loan owner. Grant this permission only to trusted operators.

params.beneficiary: The address that will receive the borrowed funds and the tokens resulting from fee payments.
params.collateralCountToAdd: The amount of collateral to add to the new loan from your balance.
params.collateralCountToTransfer: The amount of collateral to transfer from the original loan.
params.loanId: The ID of the loan to reallocate collateral from.
params.minBorrowAmount: The minimum amount to borrow, denominated in `token`.
params.prepaidFeePercent: The fee percent to charge upfront for the new loan.
params.token: The token of the new loan. Must match the existing loan's source token.

returns.newLoan: The new loan created from reallocating collateral.
returns.newLoanId: The ID of the new loan.
returns.reallocatedLoanId: The ID of the reallocated (reduced) loan.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

repayLoan(uint256,uint256,uint256,addres.details: A delegated operator (with REPAY_LOAN permission) can set `beneficiary` to any address, directing returned collateral tokens away from the loan owner. Grant this permission only to trusted operators.

params.allowance: An allowance to facilitate permit2 interactions.
params.beneficiary: The address to receive the returned collateral and any tokens resulting from paying fees.
params.collateralCountToReturn: The amount of collateral to return from the loan.
params.loanId: The ID of the loan to repay.
params.maxRepayBorrowAmount: The maximum amount to repay, denominated in the token of the source's accounting context.

returns.paidOffLoanId: The ID of the loan after repayment.

params.loanId: The loan ID to look up.

safeTransferFrom(address,address,uint256.details: Safely transfers `tokenId` token from `from` to `to`, checking first that contract recipients are aware of the ERC-721 protocol to prevent tokens from being forever locked. Requirements: - `from` cannot be the zero address. - `to` cannot be the zero address. - `tokenId` token must exist and be owned by `from`. - If the caller is not `from`, it must have been allowed to move this token by either {approve} or   {setApprovalForAll}. - If `to` refers to a smart contract, it must implement {IERC721Receiver-onERC721Received}, which is called upon   a safe transfer. Emits a {Transfer} event.

Safely transfers `tokenId` token from `from` to `to`. Requirements: - `from` cannot be the zero address. - `to` cannot be the zero address. - `tokenId` token must exist and be owned by `from`. - If the caller is not `from`, it must be approved to move this token by either {approve} or {setApprovalForAll}. - If `to` refers to a smart contract, it must implement {IERC721Receiver-onERC721Received}, which is called upon   a safe transfer. Emits a {Transfer} event.

setApprovalForAll(address,bool).details: Approve or remove `operator` as an operator for the caller. Operators can call {transferFrom} or {safeTransferFrom} for any token owned by the caller. Requirements: - The `operator` cannot be the address zero. Emits an {ApprovalForAll} event.

params.resolver: The address of the new resolver.

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

params.loanId: The ID of the loan to get the URI for.

returns._0: The token URI for the provided `loanId`.

transferFrom(address,address,uint256).details: Transfers `tokenId` token from `from` to `to`. WARNING: Note that the caller is responsible to confirm that the recipient is capable of receiving ERC-721 or else they may be permanently lost. Usage of {safeTransferFrom} prevents loss, though the caller must understand this adds an external call which potentially creates a reentrancy vulnerability. Requirements: - `from` cannot be the zero address. - `to` cannot be the zero address. - `tokenId` token must be owned by `from`. - If the caller is not `from`, it must be approved to move this token by either {approve} or {setApprovalForAll}. Emits a {Transfer} event.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

trustedForwarder().details: Returns the address of the trusted forwarder.

CONTROLLER().notice: The controller of revnets that use this loans contract.

DIRECTORY().notice: The directory of terminals and controllers for revnets.

LOAN_LIQUIDATION_DURATION().notice: The duration after which an unrepaid loan expires and its collateral is permanently lost (10 years).

MAX_PREPAID_FEE_PERCENT().notice: The maximum fee percent that can be prepaid when borrowing (50%), in terms of JBConstants.MAX_FEE.

MIN_PREPAID_FEE_PERCENT().notice: The minimum fee percent that must be prepaid when borrowing (2.5%), in terms of JBConstants.MAX_FEE.

PERMIT2().notice: The Permit2 contract used for token approvals and transfers.

PRICES().notice: A contract that stores prices for each revnet.

REV_ID().notice: The ID of the REV revnet that will receive the fees.

REV_PREPAID_FEE_PERCENT().notice: The fee percent charged by the $REV revnet on each loan (1%), in terms of JBConstants.MAX_FEE.

SUCKER_REGISTRY().notice: The sucker registry used to discover peer chain suckers for cross-chain awareness.

TERMINAL().notice: The canonical payout terminal that holds revnet treasury balances and sources all revnet loans.

borrowFrom(uint256,address,uint256,uint2.notice: Open a loan by borrowing from a revnet.

borrowableAmountFrom(uint256,uint256,uin.notice: The amount that can be borrowed from a revnet.

determineSourceFeeAmount((uint112,uint11.notice: Determines the source fee amount for a loan when paying off a certain amount.

isLoanSourceOf(uint256,address).notice: An indication if a revnet currently has outstanding loans from the specified token source.

liquidateExpiredLoansFrom(uint256,uint25.notice: Liquidate loans that have exceeded the 10-year liquidation duration.

loanOf(uint256).notice: Get a loan's full details -- amount, collateral, creation time, prepaid fee, and source.

loanSourceTokensOf(uint256).notice: The source tokens of each revnet's loans.

reallocateCollateralFromLoan(uint256,uin.notice: Refinance a loan by transferring extra collateral from an existing loan to a new loan.

repayLoan(uint256,uint256,uint256,addres.notice: Repay a loan or return excess collateral no longer needed to support the loan.

revnetIdOfLoanWith(uint256).notice: The revnet ID for a given loan ID.

setTokenUriResolver(address).notice: Set the address of the resolver used to retrieve the tokenURI of loans.

tokenURI(uint256).notice: Returns the URI where the ERC-721 standard JSON of a loan is hosted.

tokenUriResolver().notice: The contract resolving each project ID to its ERC721 URI.

totalBorrowedFrom(uint256,address).notice: The total amount loaned out by a revnet from a specified token source.

totalCollateralOf(uint256).notice: The total amount of collateral supporting a revnet's loans.

totalLoansBorrowedFor(uint256).notice: The cumulative number of loans ever created for a revnet, used as a loan ID sequence counter.
